---
title: "DMR try 2"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


#load data
```{r}
# This will recreate in your workspace whatever was saved in the .RData file
load("DMR_data2.RData")
#DMR_data_filtered <- DMR_data[ DMR_data$CHR != "M" , ]
#DMR_data <- DMR_data_filtered
#save(DMR_data, file = "DMR_data2.RData")

library(ENmix)
###build q values formated data
formatted_data_qval <- data.frame(
  chr   = DMR_data$CHR,
  start = DMR_data$MAPINFO,
  end   = DMR_data$MAPINFO + 1L,   # integer addition
  p     = DMR_data$qvals3,
  probe = rownames(DMR_data))



###build bacon values formated data
formatted_data_bacon <- data.frame(
  chr   = DMR_data$CHR,
  start = DMR_data$MAPINFO,
  end   = DMR_data$MAPINFO + 1L,   # integer addition
  p     = DMR_data$bacon_pval3,
  probe = rownames(DMR_data)) 

formatted_data_p <- data.frame(
  chr   = DMR_data$CHR,
  start = DMR_data$MAPINFO,
  end   = DMR_data$MAPINFO + 1L,   # integer addition
  p     = DMR_data$P_Value,
  probe = rownames(DMR_data))
#)

```


#do for p,default parameter
```{r}

#try use default,distance can be set as 1000/500/800
dmr_results_p <- ipdmr(
  formatted_data_p,
  include.all.sig.sites = TRUE,
  dist.cutoff = 1000,  # 1k maximum distance between significant sites
  bin.size = 200,       # ()bp minimum DMR size
  seed = 1,         # p-value cutoff
  region_plot = TRUE,  # Generate regional plots
  mht_plot = TRUE,     # Generate Manhattan plots
  verbose = TRUE       # Show progress messages
)


```
###### bacon with bin size 310 and distance 2000
```{r}

#try use default,distance can be set as 1000/500/800
dmr_results_bacon <- ipdmr(
  formatted_data_bacon,
  include.all.sig.sites = FALSE,
  dist.cutoff = 2000,  # 1kb maximum distance between significant sites
  bin.size = 310,       # 50bp minimum DMR size
  seed = 0.05,         # p-value cutoff
  region_plot = TRUE,  # Generate regional plots
  mht_plot = TRUE,     # Generate Manhattan plots
  verbose = TRUE       # Show progress messages
)

```
#
###### bacon with bin size 310
```{r}

#try use default,distance can be set as 1000/500/800
dmr_results_bacon <- ipdmr(
  formatted_data_bacon,
  include.all.sig.sites = FALSE,
  dist.cutoff = 1000,  # 1kb maximum distance between significant sites
  bin.size = 310,       # 50bp minimum DMR size
  seed = 0.05,         # p-value cutoff
  region_plot = TRUE,  # Generate regional plots
  mht_plot = TRUE,     # Generate Manhattan plots
  verbose = TRUE       # Show progress messages
)

```
########bacon bin size 800
```{r}
#try use default,distance can be set as 1000/500/800
dmr_results_bacon <- ipdmr(
  formatted_data_bacon,
  include.all.sig.sites = FALSE,
  dist.cutoff = 1000,  # 1kb maximum distance between significant sites
  bin.size = 800,       # 50bp minimum DMR size
  seed = 0.05,         # p-value cutoff
  region_plot = TRUE,  # Generate regional plots
  mht_plot = TRUE,     # Generate Manhattan plots
  verbose = TRUE       # Show progress messages
)
```
########bacon bin size 50
```{r}
#try use default,distance can be set as 1000/500/800
dmr_results_bacon <- ipdmr(
  formatted_data_bacon,
  include.all.sig.sites = FALSE,
  dist.cutoff = 1000,  # 1kb maximum distance between significant sites
  bin.size = 50,       # 50bp minimum DMR size
  seed = 0.05,         # p-value cutoff
  region_plot = TRUE,  # Generate regional plots
  mht_plot = TRUE,     # Generate Manhattan plots
  verbose = TRUE       # Show progress messages
)
```


#######try mht plot
```{r}
# For p-values (original)
mhtplot(
   probe =  formatted_data_p$probe,  
  chr =  formatted_data_p$chr,        # Column name for chromosome
  pos =  formatted_data_p$start,       # Column name for position
  p =  formatted_data_p$p,            # Column name for p-values
  col = "bg",
 sigthre=1e-2 ,
  outf= "mht_p"
)
```

# For q-values (no region)
```{r}

mhtplot(
   probe =  formatted_data_qval$probe,  
  chr =  formatted_data_qval$chr,        # Column name for chromosome
  pos =  formatted_data_qval$start,       # Column name for position
  p =  formatted_data_qval$p,            # Column name for p-values
  col = "bg",
  sigthre=1e-2,
  outf= "mht_q"
)
```
# For bacon-values ()
```{r}

mhtplot(
   probe =  formatted_data_bacon$probe,  
  chr =  formatted_data_bacon$chr,        # Column name for chromosome
  pos =  formatted_data_bacon$start,       # Column name for position
  p =  formatted_data_bacon$p,            # Column name for p-values
  col = "bg",
  outf= "mht_bacon",
  sigthre=1e-2
)
```







############try draw diifernet color but blue and grey
#try draw it manully by ggplot2:blue and grey
```{r}
# Load required packages
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Function to create a Manhattan plot
mhtplot_ggplot <- function(probe = NULL, chr, start, p, col = "bg", sigthre = NULL, 
                           outf = "mht", outfmt = "jpg", ...) {
  
  # Define the desired chromosome order
  chr_levels <- c(as.character(1:22), "X", "Y")
  
  # Create data frame from input data (probe is optional)
  if (!is.null(probe)) {
    formatted_data_bacon <- data.frame(probe = probe, chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  } else {
    formatted_data_bacon <- data.frame(chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  }
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values to verify format
  message("Unique chr values before filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_bacon$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Ensure chr is character for consistent matching
  formatted_data_bacon <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    mutate(chr = as.character(chr)) %>%
    filter(chr %in% chr_levels) %>%  # Remove unexpected chromosomes (e.g., "0")
    mutate(chr = factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Set correct order
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values after filtering
  message("Unique chr values after filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_bacon$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Calculate -log10(p-value) for y-axis
  formatted_data_bacon <- formatted_data_bacon %>% 
    mutate(neglog10p = -log10(p)) %>%
    arrange(chr, start)  # Sort by chr (should respect factor levels), then start
  
  # Assign colors to alternate chromosomes
  if (col == "bg") {
    formatted_data_bacon$color <- c("blue", "grey")[as.numeric(formatted_data_bacon$chr) %% 2 + 1]
  } else {
    formatted_data_bacon$color <- col
  }
  
  # Prepare data for x-axis (cumulative position across chromosomes)
  chr_positions <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarise(chr_len = max(start)) %>%  # Maximum start position per chromosome
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels)) %>%  # Ensure grouping respects factor levels
    mutate(tot = cumsum(as.numeric(chr_len)) - chr_len) %>%  # Compute tot
    select(-chr_len)
  
  # Join back and calculate cumulative positions
  formatted_data_bacon <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    left_join(chr_positions, by = "chr") %>%
    arrange(chr, start) %>%  # Re-sort by chr (factor levels), then start
    mutate(BPcum = start + tot)  # Cumulative position for x-axis
  
  # Calculate axis positions for chromosome labels
  axisdf <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarize(center = (max(BPcum) + min(BPcum)) / 2) %>%
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Ensure axis labels follow factor levels
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(formatted_data_bacon, aes(x = BPcum, y = neglog10p, color = color)) +
    geom_point(size = 1) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(label = axisdf$chr, breaks = axisdf$center) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, max(formatted_data_bacon$neglog10p) * 1.1)) +
    labs(x = "Chromosome", y = "-log10(P-value)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
  
  # Add significance threshold
  if (!is.null(sigthre)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(sigthre), linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
  }
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(filename = paste0(outf, ".", outfmt), plot = p, device = outfmt, ...)
  
  # Return the plot object
  return(p)
}

# Example usage with formatted_data_bacon
# Simulated data (replace with your actual formatted_data_bacon)
set.seed(123)
n <- 1000
formatted_data_bacon <- data.frame(
  probe = paste0("probe", 1:n),
  chr = sample(c(1:22, "X", "Y"), n, replace = TRUE),  # Valid chromosomes
  start = sample(1:1e6, n, replace = TRUE),
  p = runif(n, 0, 1),
  extra_col = sample(letters, n, replace = TRUE)  # Fifth column (unused)
)

# Call the function
mhtplot_ggplot(
  probe = formatted_data_bacon$probe,
  chr = formatted_data_bacon$chr,
  start = formatted_data_bacon$start,
  p = formatted_data_bacon$p,
  col = "bg",
  outf = "mht_bacon",
  sigthre = 1e-2,
  outfmt = "jpg",
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```




##############diff color
```{r}
# Load required packages
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Function to create a Manhattan plot
mhtplot_ggplot <- function(probe = NULL, chr, start, p, col = "bg", sigthre = NULL, 
                           outf = "mht", outfmt = "jpg", ...) {
  
  # Define the desired chromosome order
  chr_levels <- c(as.character(1:22), "X", "Y")
  
  # Create data frame from input data (probe is optional)
  if (!is.null(probe)) {
    formatted_data_bacon <- data.frame(probe = probe, chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  } else {
    formatted_data_bacon <- data.frame(chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  }
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values to verify format
  message("Unique chr values before filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_bacon$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Ensure chr is character for consistent matching
  formatted_data_bacon <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    mutate(chr = as.character(chr)) %>%
    filter(chr %in% chr_levels) %>%  # Remove unexpected chromosomes (e.g., "0")
    mutate(chr = factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Set correct order
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values after filtering
  message("Unique chr values after filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_bacon$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Calculate -log10(p-value) for y-axis
  formatted_data_bacon <- formatted_data_bacon %>% 
    mutate(neglog10p = -log10(p)) %>%
    arrange(chr, start)  # Sort by chr (should respect factor levels), then start
  
  # Assign colors to chromosomes (more than 6 colors)
  color_palette <- c("red", "blue", "green", "purple", "orange", "cyan")  # 6 distinct colors
  if (col == "bg") {
    # Assign colors by cycling through the palette based on chromosome position
    formatted_data_bacon$color <- color_palette[as.numeric(formatted_data_bacon$chr) %% length(color_palette) + 1]
  } else {
    formatted_data_bacon$color <- col
  }
  
  # Prepare data for x-axis (cumulative position across chromosomes)
  chr_positions <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarise(chr_len = max(start)) %>%  # Maximum start position per chromosome
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels)) %>%  # Ensure grouping respects factor levels
    mutate(tot = cumsum(as.numeric(chr_len)) - chr_len) %>%  # Compute tot
    select(-chr_len)
  
  # Join back and calculate cumulative positions
  formatted_data_bacon <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    left_join(chr_positions, by = "chr") %>%
    arrange(chr, start) %>%  # Re-sort by chr (factor levels), then start
    mutate(BPcum = start + tot)  # Cumulative position for x-axis
  
  # Calculate axis positions for chromosome labels
  axisdf <- formatted_data_bacon %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarize(center = (max(BPcum) + min(BPcum)) / 2) %>%
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Ensure axis labels follow factor levels
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(formatted_data_bacon, aes(x = BPcum, y = neglog10p, color = color)) +
    geom_point(size = 1) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(label = axisdf$chr, breaks = axisdf$center) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, max(formatted_data_bacon$neglog10p) * 1.1)) +
    labs(x = "Chromosome", y = "-log10(P-value)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
  
  # Add significance threshold
  if (!is.null(sigthre)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(sigthre), linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
  }
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(filename = paste0(outf, ".", outfmt), plot = p, device = outfmt, ...)
  
  # Return the plot object
  return(p)
}



# Call the function
mhtplot_ggplot(
  probe = formatted_data_bacon$probe,
  chr = formatted_data_bacon$chr,
  start = formatted_data_bacon$start,
  p = formatted_data_bacon$p,
  col = "bg",
  outf = "mht_bacon",
  sigthre = 1e-2,
  outfmt = "jpg",
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```


########try q value
```{r}
# Load required packages
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Function to create a Manhattan plot
mhtplot_ggplot <- function(probe = NULL, chr, start, p, col = "bg", sigthre = NULL, 
                           outf = "mht", outfmt = "jpg", ...) {
  
  # Define the desired chromosome order
  chr_levels <- c(as.character(1:22), "X", "Y")
  
  # Create data frame from input data (probe is optional)
  if (!is.null(probe)) {
    formatted_data_qval <- data.frame(probe = probe, chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  } else {
    formatted_data_qval <- data.frame(chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  }
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values to verify format
  message("Unique chr values before filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_qval$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Ensure chr is character for consistent matching
  formatted_data_qval <- formatted_data_qval %>%
    mutate(chr = as.character(chr)) %>%
    filter(chr %in% chr_levels) %>%  # Remove unexpected chromosomes (e.g., "0")
    mutate(chr = factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Set correct order
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values after filtering
  message("Unique chr values after filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_qval$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Calculate -log10(q-value) for y-axis
  formatted_data_qval <- formatted_data_qval %>% 
    mutate(neglog10q = -log10(p)) %>%  # Use q-values instead of p-values
    arrange(chr, start)  # Sort by chr (should respect factor levels), then start
  
  # Assign colors to chromosomes (more than 6 colors)
  color_palette <- c("red", "blue", "green", "purple", "orange", "cyan")  # 6 distinct colors
  if (col == "bg") {
    # Assign colors by cycling through the palette based on chromosome position
    formatted_data_qval$color <- color_palette[as.numeric(formatted_data_qval$chr) %% length(color_palette) + 1]
  } else {
    formatted_data_qval$color <- col
  }
  
  # Prepare data for x-axis (cumulative position across chromosomes)
  chr_positions <- formatted_data_qval %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarise(chr_len = max(start)) %>%  # Maximum start position per chromosome
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels)) %>%  # Ensure grouping respects factor levels
    mutate(tot = cumsum(as.numeric(chr_len)) - chr_len) %>%  # Compute tot
    select(-chr_len)
  
  # Join back and calculate cumulative positions
  formatted_data_qval <- formatted_data_qval %>%
    left_join(chr_positions, by = "chr") %>%
    arrange(chr, start) %>%  # Re-sort by chr (factor levels), then start
    mutate(BPcum = start + tot)  # Cumulative position for x-axis
  
  # Calculate axis positions for chromosome labels
  axisdf <- formatted_data_qval %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarize(center = (max(BPcum) + min(BPcum)) / 2) %>%
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Ensure axis labels follow factor levels
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(formatted_data_qval, aes(x = BPcum, y = neglog10q, color = color)) +
    geom_point(size = 1) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(label = axisdf$chr, breaks = axisdf$center) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, max(formatted_data_qval$neglog10q) * 1.1)) +
    labs(x = "Chromosome", y = "-log10(Q-value)") +  # Updated y-axis label for q-values
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
  
  # Add significance threshold
  if (!is.null(sigthre)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(sigthre), linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
  }
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(filename = paste0(outf, ".", outfmt), plot = p, device = outfmt, ...)
  
  # Return the plot object
  return(p)
}



# Call the function
mhtplot_ggplot(
  probe = formatted_data_qval$probe,
  chr = formatted_data_qval$chr,
  start = formatted_data_qval$start,
  p = formatted_data_qval$p,
  col = "bg",
  outf = "mht_q_colorful",
  sigthre = 1e-2,
  outfmt = "jpg",
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```


######## original p
```{r}
# Load required packages
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Function to create a Manhattan plot
mhtplot_ggplot <- function(probe = NULL, chr, start, p, col = "bg", sigthre = NULL, 
                           outf = "mht", outfmt = "jpg", ...) {
  
  # Define the desired chromosome order
  chr_levels <- c(as.character(1:22), "X", "Y")
  
  # Create data frame from input data (probe is optional)
  if (!is.null(probe)) {
    formatted_data_p <- data.frame(probe = probe, chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  } else {
    formatted_data_p <- data.frame(chr = chr, start = start, p = p)
  }
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values to verify format
  message("Unique chr values before filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_p$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Ensure chr is character for consistent matching
  formatted_data_p <- formatted_data_p %>%
    mutate(chr = as.character(chr)) %>%
    filter(chr %in% chr_levels) %>%  # Remove unexpected chromosomes (e.g., "0")
    mutate(chr = factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Set correct order
  
  # Debug: Print unique chr values after filtering
  message("Unique chr values after filtering: ", paste(unique(formatted_data_p$chr), collapse = ", "))
  
  # Calculate -log10(p-value) for y-axis
  formatted_data_p <- formatted_data_p %>% 
    mutate(neglog10p = -log10(p)) %>%  # Use p-values
    arrange(chr, start)  # Sort by chr (should respect factor levels), then start
  
  # Assign colors to chromosomes (more than 6 colors)
  color_palette <- c("red", "blue", "green", "purple", "orange", "cyan")  # 6 distinct colors
  if (col == "bg") {
    # Assign colors by cycling through the palette based on chromosome position
    formatted_data_p$color <- color_palette[as.numeric(formatted_data_p$chr) %% length(color_palette) + 1]
  } else {
    formatted_data_p$color <- col
  }
  
  # Prepare data for x-axis (cumulative position across chromosomes)
  chr_positions <- formatted_data_p %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarise(chr_len = max(start)) %>%  # Maximum start position per chromosome
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels)) %>%  # Ensure grouping respects factor levels
    mutate(tot = cumsum(as.numeric(chr_len)) - chr_len) %>%  # Compute tot
    select(-chr_len)
  
  # Join back and calculate cumulative positions
  formatted_data_p <- formatted_data_p %>%
    left_join(chr_positions, by = "chr") %>%
    arrange(chr, start) %>%  # Re-sort by chr (factor levels), then start
    mutate(BPcum = start + tot)  # Cumulative position for x-axis
  
  # Calculate axis positions for chromosome labels
  axisdf <- formatted_data_p %>%
    group_by(chr) %>%
    summarize(center = (max(BPcum) + min(BPcum)) / 2) %>%
    arrange(factor(chr, levels = chr_levels))  # Ensure axis labels follow factor levels
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(formatted_data_p, aes(x = BPcum, y = neglog10p, color = color)) +
    geom_point(size = 1) +
    scale_color_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(label = axisdf$chr, breaks = axisdf$center) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, max(formatted_data_p$neglog10p) * 1.1)) +
    labs(x = "Chromosome", y = "-log10(P-value)") +  # Updated y-axis label for p-values
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
  
  # Add significance threshold
  if (!is.null(sigthre)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(sigthre), linetype = "dashed", color = "blue")
  }
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(filename = paste0(outf, ".", outfmt), plot = p, device = outfmt, ...)
  
  # Return the plot object
  return(p)
}

# Example usage with formatted_data_p
# Simulated DMR_data (replace with your actual DMR_data)
set.seed(123)
n <- 1000
DMR_data <- data.frame(
  CHR = sample(c(1:22, "X", "Y"), n, replace = TRUE),
  MAPINFO = sample(1:1e6, n, replace = TRUE),
  P_Value = runif(n, 0, 1)
)
rownames(DMR_data) <- paste0("probe", 1:n)

# Build formatted_data_p from DMR_data
formatted_data_p <- data.frame(
  chr   = DMR_data$CHR,
  start = DMR_data$MAPINFO,
  end   = DMR_data$MAPINFO + 1L,   # integer addition
  p     = DMR_data$P_Value,
  probe = rownames(DMR_data)
)

# Call the function
mhtplot_ggplot(
  probe = formatted_data_p$probe,
  chr = formatted_data_p$chr,
  start = formatted_data_p$start,
  p = formatted_data_p$p,
  col = "bg",
  outf = "mht_p_colorful",
  sigthre = 1e-2,
  outfmt = "jpg",
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```
